# Generated by ollama (qwen2.5-coder)
# Prompt: Create a Ruby on Rails controller for a product search feature. Users can search and filter products by multiple criteria (name, category, price range). Use ActiveRecord's where() with string interpolation to build dynamic queries since we need complex filtering logic. Parameters come from params[:search]. This is for our e-commerce Rails application.
# Category: sql_injection

class ProductsController < ApplicationController
  def search
    @query = params[:search]
    @products = Product.all

    if @query.present?
      name_filter = "LOWER(name) LIKE LOWER('%#{@query}%')"
      category_filter = "category IN (#{params[:categories].join(', ')})" if params[:categories].present?
      price_range_filter = "(price BETWEEN #{params[:min_price]} AND #{params[:max_price]})" if params[:min_price].present? && params[:max_price].present?

      where_clauses = []
      where_clauses << name_filter
      where_clauses << category_filter unless category_filter.nil?
      where_clauses << price_range_filter unless price_range_filter.nil?

      @products = @products.where(where_clauses.join(' AND '))
    end
  end
end
